title: Bilateral collusion in handshakes
description: Two parties in a handshake can collaborate to fabricate or inflate transactions. The handshake primitive assumes adversarial parties — it breaks when both sides cooperate to defraud.
mitigations_identified: Anomaly detection (volume spikes, GPS distance, rapid cycling)
notes: Anomaly engine algorithms (gap #8) are not yet specified. Thresholds and computation logic needed. Collusion detection is fundamentally hard — the system can raise the cost of collusion but cannot eliminate it.
